# Figuring Out Pharma
> A pharma editorial blog written for B.Pharma students and freshers in India who want to understand how the pharmaceutical industry actually works - not how textbooks say it does.

The site covers pharma marketing, drug pricing, brand strategy, regulatory affairs, careers, and industry history. All content is written in plain language with no assumed prior knowledge. Published by a final-year B.Pharma student based in Mumbai.

URL: https://figuringoutpharma.com

---

## Articles

### FMCG vs pharma marketing — explained from scratch, no assumptions
URL: https://figuringoutpharma.com/articles/fmcg-vs-pharma-marketing
Topic: pharma marketing, FMCG marketing, prescription drugs, OTC marketing, brand strategy India, medical representatives
Summary: A full breakdown of how FMCG and pharmaceutical marketing differ across four dimensions — audiences, brand lifespan, advertising restrictions, and pricing. Explains why pharma cannot advertise prescription drugs in mass media in India, how the patent cliff creates a fundamentally different brand lifecycle, and where the two industries converge at Rx-to-OTC switches.

### How drugs are priced in India — the real explanation
URL: https://figuringoutpharma.com/articles/how-drugs-are-priced-in-india
Topic: DPCO, drug pricing India, NLEM, NPPA, trade margins, chemist incentives, FDC loophole
Summary: Explains how the Drug Prices Control Order works in India, how the NPPA calculates ceiling prices using a market-based formula rather than production costs, and the loopholes companies use including FDC combinations and strength variations. Covers trade margin structures and why brand price variations for the same molecule can range from 1000 to 4000 percent.

### How Crocin turned a generic molecule into a household brand
URL: https://figuringoutpharma.com/articles/how-crocin-became-a-household-brand
Topic: Crocin, Dolo 650, paracetamol brand strategy, OTC marketing India, DPCO loophole, pharma branding
Summary: Explains how GSK Consumer Healthcare built Crocin into India's dominant paracetamol brand through consumer pull advertising and a dual-brand strategy with Calpol for doctors. Covers how Dolo 650 disrupted the market by exploiting the DPCO pricing gap on the 650mg formulation, and how COVID-19 amplified Dolo's prescription-led position.

### How Online Pharmacies Changed the Indian Pharma Industry
URL: https://figuringoutpharma.com/articles/how-online-pharmacies-changed-indian-pharma
Topic: e-pharmacy India, online pharmacy, Tata 1mg, PharmEasy, Netmeds, DCGI, digital pharma, E-Pharmacy Rules
Summary: Covers the rise of e-pharmacies in India from a 2 percent niche to a multi-billion dollar industry, the four business models in use, why consumers switched based on perceived safety rather than price alone, and what e-pharmacies did to the AIOCD retail cartel. Explains why the E-Pharmacy Rules drafted in 2018 have still not been notified into law as of 2026.

### How medical reps are actually trained — and what they are really selling
URL: https://figuringoutpharma.com/articles/how-medical-reps-are-trained
Topic: medical representative, MR training, pharma field force, PCA framework, UCPMP 2024, pharma careers India
Summary: Explains the Promotion-Conversion-Availability training framework that governs how medical reps are trained in India. Covers why scientific depth drives prescriber influence more than gifts or samples, the concept of physician mindshare as the real commercial asset, and what UCPMP 2024 means for the field force going forward.

### Schedule M explained — the biggest regulatory shift in Indian pharma in decades
URL: https://figuringoutpharma.com/articles/schedule-m-regulatory-shift-explained
Topic: Schedule M, GMP India, CDSCO, DCGI, pharmaceutical manufacturing, quality risk management, MSME pharma
Summary: Explains the shift from the rule-based 2001 Schedule M to the risk-based revised Schedule M requiring Pharmaceutical Quality System and Quality Risk Management. Covers what changed on the factory floor, the phased compliance deadlines, and why most MSME units did not apply for the 2025 extension. Enforcement status as of 2026 is addressed.

### Dolo 650 uses, dosage and side effects — explained
URL: https://figuringoutpharma.com/articles/dolo-650-uses-dosage-side-effects
Topic: Dolo 650, paracetamol dosage, paracetamol side effects, NLEM, DPCO, drug pricing India, acetaminophen India
Summary: Explains what Dolo 650 is, its clinical uses for fever and pain relief, the correct adult dosage table, liver toxicity risks, pregnancy and breastfeeding safety guidance, and the pharmacological difference from Crocin. Covers the DPCO pricing arbitrage that caused the 650mg dose to dominate India's paracetamol market and the 2022 NLEM revision that brought it under price control.

---

## Case Studies

### The Vioxx Withdrawal — blockbuster economics, selective science, and the fall of a $20 billion drug
URL: https://figuringoutpharma.com/articles/vioxx-withdrawal
Topic: Vioxx, rofecoxib, Merck, COX-2 inhibitor, drug safety, pharmacovigilance, FDA Sentinel Initiative, clinical trial registration
Summary: Explains what COX-2 inhibitors were solving and the VIGOR trial's four-fold cardiovascular risk signal that Merck managed through the Naproxen Hypothesis. Covers how the commercial machinery used training scripts for reps and ghostwritten journals to suppress the signal, why the APPROVe placebo-controlled trial made denial impossible, and what permanently changed including the FDA Sentinel Initiative and mandatory clinical trial registration.

### Ranbaxy and the FDA — How India's biggest pharma success story collapsed
URL: https://figuringoutpharma.com/articles/ranbaxy-and-the-fda
Topic: Ranbaxy, FDA, data fraud, generic drugs, GMP, bioequivalence, whistleblower, Dinesh Thakur, GDUFA
Summary: Covers Ranbaxy's rise from Amritsar distributor to the world's third-largest generic manufacturer and the whistleblower investigation by Dinesh Thakur that uncovered systematic fabrication of bioequivalence and stability data. Explains the FDA's Application Integrity Policy, the 500 million dollar criminal settlement in 2013, and how the case permanently changed generic drug auditing globally.

### The Thalidomide Tragedy — and the untold Indian chapter
URL: https://figuringoutpharma.com/articles/thalidomide-tragedy
Topic: thalidomide, drug safety history, Frances Kelsey, FDA, Kefauver-Harris Amendments, teratogenicity, Indian pharma history, Goa
Summary: Full timeline of the thalidomide crisis including Frances Kelsey's FDA refusal and the specific fetal vulnerability window between gestational days 20 and 36. Covers India's complicated story where Goa as a Portuguese colony received thalidomide shipments in 1959 while mainland India blocked it. Explains what the Kefauver-Harris Amendments of 1962 permanently changed about drug approval worldwide.

### The Bayer–Monsanto Merger — how a $63 billion agricultural bet destroyed a pharmaceutical legacy
URL: https://figuringoutpharma.com/articles/bayer-monsanto-merger
Topic: Bayer, Monsanto, merger, glyphosate, Roundup, pharma pipeline, capital allocation, shareholder revolt
Summary: Covers Bayer's pre-deal pipeline problem and Werner Baumann's unsolicited 63 billion dollar all-cash bid that bypassed a shareholder vote through debt financing. Explains how the glyphosate litigation produced over 17 billion dollars in provisions, how agricultural liabilities starved the pharmaceutical R&D pipeline, and the historic 55 percent shareholder vote against management in 2019.

### Lipitor's Patent Cliff — how the world's best-selling drug lost everything overnight
URL: https://figuringoutpharma.com/articles/lipitor-patent-cliff
Topic: Lipitor, atorvastatin, Pfizer, patent cliff, generic drugs, lifecycle management, pay-for-delay, Ranbaxy
Summary: Covers how Lipitor became the world's best-selling drug and Pfizer's pre-cliff strategies including a 17.5 percent price increase in the final year and one billion dollars annually in direct-to-consumer advertising. Explains the pay-for-delay settlement with Ranbaxy in 2008 and the three defensive strategies Pfizer executed on the day the patent expired in November 2011.

### COVID-19 Vaccine Development — how pharma did the impossible
URL: https://figuringoutpharma.com/articles/covid-vaccine-development
Topic: COVID-19 vaccines, mRNA vaccines, Covishield, Covaxin, Serum Institute, Bharat Biotech, rolling review, vaccine development India
Summary: Explains why vaccines normally take 10 to 15 years and what structurally changed during COVID through parallel development funded by government advance market commitments. Covers the three platform technologies, how rolling regulatory review worked, India's specific role with Serum Institute producing over 200 million doses per month, and what permanently changed in platform science and supply chains.

---

## Site Information

Published: June 2026
Author: Figuring Out Pharma (B.Pharma student, Mumbai, India)
Primary audience: B.Pharma students and freshers entering the Indian pharmaceutical industry
Content types: Long-form articles (5-12 min read), pharma case studies (10-11 min read)
Topics covered: Pharma marketing, drug pricing, brand strategy, regulatory affairs, pharma careers, industry history, global case studies
Contact: contact@figuringoutpharma.com
